title: Reference Counting, Weak References, and Cycles · C Contract Brief: Cycle Leak Diagnosis
summary: A document graph shares nodes across views and leaks after parent-child cycles appear. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem cycle leak diagnosis increment in overflow-safe reference-counted graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.
Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Cycle Leak Diagnosis · Theory Cycle Leak Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9426. Produce bounded C evidence to model strong counts and expose a cycle that cannot reach zero; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cycle Leak Diagnosis contract for the supplied data.
